title: bradleia obliqua
intro: Bradleia obliqua, also known as the Red Samoan Wood tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Pentaphyllaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in Samoan medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and skin conditions, due to its medicinal properties which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Bradleia obliqua include reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and promoting wound healing. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by boiling the leaves in water to create a tea, or by infusing the bark in oil to create a topical application.
